_comment.1: SOUNDCARD actuation-latency test. The soundcard line-out drives the
_comment.2: y-FINE input (where the piplate y-fine wire used to go, divided by ~16).
_comment.3: latency_test outputs [y,x]; aylp_alsa sends vector[0]->L, vector[1]->R.
_comment.4: index_cmd 0 steps the element that drives soundcard L; set 1 for R (we
_comment.5: do not know which channel is wired to y-fine, so run BOTH). index_err 0
_comment.6: = watch CoM y (the axis actually driven). Whichever channel moves y is
_comment.7: the connected one; the other reads a ~0 step in calibration.
_comment.8: AC-COUPLING: the step decays back toward baseline, so the calibration
_comment.9: 'settled step' may be small and the 50% crossing skewed -- read the
_comment.10: DEPARTURE (first motion) for the pure transport delay.
_comment.11: ACTUATOR is the low-latency threaded aylp_alsa (0.083 ms queue). The
_comment.12: piplate stays PARKED (scale 0) to hold coarse + x-fine at 2 V bias so
_comment.13: the beam sits on-sensor while only the soundcard moves y. 96x96 ROI +
_comment.14: acquire because the y rest position shifts when the AC-coupled soundcard
_comment.15: replaces the piplate's 2 V y bias. NOTE: 96 rows lowers the frame rate
_comment.16: (~1260 Hz) and adds readout latency vs the 32-row loop -- fine for a
_comment.17: first delay look; the reported ms includes it.
_comment.18: Run L then R: build/anyloop -l info contrib/conf_latency_soundcard.json

params._roi_note: 32x32 (operating ROI = ~3788 Hz, minimum readout delay) centred on the CURRENT beam CoM (sensor x290,y395, peak 167) at the soundcard-driven y rest position -- located by contrib/find_roi.py 2026-07-22 (beam lands ~18,16 in-window). Re-run find_roi.py if the beam moves.

pipeline._note: index_cmd 0 -> soundcard L; change to 1 for R. index_err 0 = watch CoM y. out_size 2 = [y,x]. low/high +-1.0 = full-scale soundcard drive (through the ~16x divider it is small, so use the whole range). period 0.15 s: short enough that the AC-coupled response has not fully decayed within a half-cycle. warmup 35 s > piplate start_delay 30 + CoM acquire 5.

SOUNDCARD actuator, low-latency threaded (0.083 ms queue). scale [1,1]: full-scale on both channels; the driven element is set by latency_test index_cmd. rt_prio 80 needs privilege (falls back to normal priority with a warning otherwise).

Piplate PARKED: scale [0,0,0,0] holds coarse (ch2,ch3) + x-fine (ch1) + the now-disconnected y-fine (ch0) at their 2 V bias so the beam stays on-sensor while only the soundcard moves y. start_delay lets coarse stabilize.
